Sliding Window.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
Sliding Window.easyApproach: Check every possible subarray using nested loops, summing its elements and tracking the smallest length whose sum is at least target.
Sliding Window.optimizedApproach: Use a sliding window with two pointers. Expand the right pointer to add elements to the running sum; whenever the sum meets or exceeds target, shrink from the left while updating the minimum length, then continue expanding.

Sliding Window.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Sliding Window.easyApproach: For every starting index, expand a substring character by character using a set to detect duplicates, tracking the longest valid length found.
Sliding Window.optimizedApproach: Maintain a sliding window with a hashmap storing the last seen index of each character. Expand the right pointer each step; if the character was seen inside the current window, move the left pointer just past its last occurrence, then update the max window length.

Sliding Window.title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
Sliding Window.easyApproach: For each starting index in s, try to match the concatenation by checking every permutation-like grouping of word-length chunks against the words list using a frequency map, restarting the check from scratch each time.
Sliding Window.optimizedApproach: Since all words share the same length, run a sliding window for each of the word-length possible offsets. Maintain a count map of needed words; slide one word-length at a time, adding the new word and removing the leftmost word when a word's count exceeds what's needed or an invalid word appears, otherwise recording valid starting indices when the window matches exactly.

Sliding Window.title: Minimum Window Substring
Sliding Window.easyApproach: For every possible substring of s, check whether it contains all characters of t with the required frequency, tracking the shortest valid one found.
Sliding Window.optimizedApproach: Use a sliding window with a frequency map of characters needed from t and a counter of how many required characters are currently satisfied. Expand the right pointer to include characters; once all requirements are met, shrink from the left to minimize the window while still valid, updating the best result throughout.
